  "design_principles": "Volatility is pooled, not eliminated. Group commitment creates discipline individuals lack. Access requires social approval, not just need.",

  "how_it_works": "Workers join cross-industry battalions. Each payment: X% to personal retirement, Y% to battalion mutual aid fund. At retirement: personal account + share of collective fund. Desertion (leaving with unfulfilled obligations) forfeits benefits. Platform technology enforces commitments, tracks obligations, facilitates voting.",
  "what_is_new": "Treats income volatility as collective defense problem, not individual savings problem. Uses social commitment as enforcement mechanism. Separates emergency buffer from retirement at group level.",
  "why_it_works": "N people's volatility largely cancels out—one person's famine is another's feast. Social pressure creates discipline willpower cannot. Mutual aid fund prevents retirement raids. Obligation to real people beats obligation to abstract future self. Cross-industry formation decorrelates risk.",

solution.label: Mutual Defense Retirement Battalions
solution.design_principles: Volatility is pooled, not eliminated. Group commitment creates discipline individuals lack. Access requires social approval, not just need.
solution.core_mechanism: Small cohorts (50-200) cross-subsidize income volatility through mandatory mutual aid contributions, with retirement access requiring group approval.
solution.how_it_works: Workers join cross-industry battalions. Each payment: X% to personal retirement, Y% to battalion mutual aid fund. Bad months: draw from mutual aid (not retirement), incur replenishment obligation. Early retirement access requires battalion majority vote. At retirement: personal account + share of collective fund. Desertion (leaving with unfulfilled obligations) forfeits benefits. Platform technology enforces commitments, tracks obligations, facilitates voting.
solution.what_is_new: Treats income volatility as collective defense problem, not individual savings problem. Uses social commitment as enforcement mechanism. Separates emergency buffer from retirement at group level.
solution.why_it_works: N people's volatility largely cancels out—one person's famine is another's feast. Social pressure creates discipline willpower cannot. Mutual aid fund prevents retirement raids. Obligation to real people beats obligation to abstract future self. Cross-industry formation decorrelates risk.
solution.why_it_fails: Requires coordination infrastructure. Free riders may exploit mutual aid. Trust between strangers hard to build. Industry-correlated battalions could collapse together. Exit restrictions feel coercive. May attract adverse selection—those who expect to need help join, those who don't avoid it.
solution.medium_term: Gig platforms integrate battalion formation. Workers develop solidarity identity. Income volatility stabilizes through pooling. Early retirement raids decrease as mutual aid absorbs shocks.
solution.long_term_vision: Retirement security fully decoupled from employer or income consistency. Worker solidarity becomes institutional infrastructure. Financial fragility of gig economy transforms into collective resilience. Battalions become multi-generational, retirees mentoring new members. A new commons emerges between market and state.
